title: Find Your Owner's Manual
situation: Alex has borrowed his mom's car and sees a dashboard button he does not recognize. Tyler suggests pressing it. Alex decides to find the owner's manual first.
intro: Every car is a little different. The owner's manual explains controls, warning lights, fuel requirements, maintenance schedules, and features that may be unfamiliar.

steps.title: Check the glove compartment
steps.text: Many cars still include a printed manual, often inside a zippered pouch with warranty and maintenance booklets.

steps.title: Visit the manufacturer's website
steps.text: Search using the car's year, make, and model. Most manufacturers provide the official manual as a free PDF.

steps.title: Use the VIN
steps.text: The Vehicle Identification Number identifies the exact car. Look through the lower driver's-side windshield or on the driver's door jamb.

steps.title: Check the dashboard screen
steps.text: Many newer vehicles include a searchable digital owner's manual in the infotainment system.

challenge: Open the glove compartment and look for the manual.
Find the VIN on the windshield or door jamb.
Save the official PDF manual on your phone.
tip: Download the PDF while you have Wi-Fi. Then it will be available even when you are away from home.
tyler_quote: I thought every car had the same buttons.
lesson: Every car really is different. Two minutes spent locating the manual can prevent a lot of guessing.
